nodes.label: extreme-proof-stack
nodes.meaning: Uses concrete facts, routines, or stats to prove a person's uncommon level

nodes.label: asdicipline--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "McGregor trained twice a day, every day, for years before his first UFC contract."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Before-fame sacrifice told through concrete training behaviors.
nodes.best_fit: Combat sports, athlete discipline, work-ethic biography

nodes.label: asdicipline--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "The most disciplined man in boxing history"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Legend claim supported by routine details, record stats, and quote closure.
nodes.best_fit: Boxing, athlete stories, discipline biography

nodes.label: legendperform--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "He ate up to 10,000 calories a day. Went 5 years without skipping a single day of training."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Absurd routine and record facts prove elite dominance.
nodes.best_fit: Athlete stories, swimming, Olympic mindset

nodes.label: legendperform--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "Over 90% of his wins are knockouts. In weight classes where knockouts almost never happen."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Surprising stat creates curiosity, then training method explains it.
nodes.best_fit: Boxing, performance analysis, sports biography
nodes.priority_reason: Surprising stat plus explanation is a reliable biography structure.

nodes.label: legendperform--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "He went 10 years without losing a fight. 154 wins in a row."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Dominance streak plus brutal fundamentals and quote closure.
nodes.best_fit: Combat sports, judo, discipline, athlete stories

nodes.label: challenge-question
nodes.meaning: Opens by asking whether the viewer could survive the standard

nodes.label: asdicipline--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "Could you train like Conor on an empty stomach?"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Viewer is challenged to survive the hidden standard behind a famous outcome.
nodes.best_fit: Athlete mindset, discipline, toughness, training sacrifice
nodes.priority_reason: Challenge-question hook works well for discipline and athlete accounts.

nodes.label: numbered-identity-rules
nodes.meaning: Turns values into a numbered code or rule set

nodes.label: asdicipline--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "David Goggins's 4 rules of insane discipline"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Named figure plus hard rules that convert discipline into a code.
nodes.best_fit: Discipline accounts, athlete mindset, no-excuse content

nodes.label: insane-habits-list
nodes.meaning: Promises behaviors most people cannot handle
nodes.portable_formula: [Person/group] [number] insane habits most people can't handle

nodes.label: asdicipline--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "McGregor 5 insane habits. Most people can't handle."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Numbered habits are framed as too intense for normal people.
nodes.best_fit: Athlete habits, combat sports, discipline, masculine self-improvement

nodes.label: science-protocol
nodes.meaning: Explains a desirable outcome through mechanisms and steps
nodes.portable_formula: How to [outcome] like a [high-status identity], according to [science/source]

nodes.label: chasingpeaks0--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "How to Learn a Language Like a PEAK Human. According to neuroscience."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Debunk common methods, explain the mechanism, then prescribe an optimized protocol.
nodes.best_fit: Study, language learning, science-athlete, learning systems

nodes.label: chasingpeaks0--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "What a Peak Human Would ACTUALLY Look Like. Complete Optimization."
nodes.transferable_pattern: "Complete optimization" series broken into morning, night, and next-part protocol.
nodes.best_fit: Health, fitness, routine design, optimization

nodes.label: chasingpeaks0--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "How You Build a PEAK HUMAN Memory. According to neuroscience."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Mechanism-first learning protocol, followed by what to stop doing.
nodes.best_fit: Study, memory, productivity, science explainers

nodes.label: user-sophia-study-study--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "How Harvard students ACTUALLY study"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Elite institution promise followed by a method list and save-for-later close.
nodes.best_fit: Study methods, exam prep, student productivity

nodes.label: self-sabotage-diagnosis
nodes.meaning: Names a hidden behavior that is killing potential
nodes.portable_formula: How you're killing [desire] without realizing it

nodes.label: chasingpeaks0--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "How You're Killing Your Potential Without Realizing It."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Hidden dopamine/noise behavior is framed as the reason motivation feels broken.
nodes.best_fit: Focus, dopamine, discipline, productivity, athlete mindset
nodes.priority_reason: Broad self-sabotage diagnosis that adapts to study, athlete, and focus niches.

nodes.label: roi-ranked-list
nodes.meaning: Ranks behaviors by payoff, often with a credibility cue

nodes.label: chasingpeaks0--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "The HIGHEST ROI Habits for Self Improvement. Ranked by science."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Habits are sorted by payoff tiers instead of listed equally.
nodes.best_fit: Self-improvement, study, fitness, lifestyle habits

nodes.label: verified-byhumans--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "5 hobbies with high biological ROI."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Masculine hobbies are justified through body/brain payoff.
nodes.best_fit: Men's self-improvement, health, confidence, hobbies

nodes.label: rapid-transformation-listicle
nodes.meaning: Promises a short-window identity or physique change, then delivers numbered tactical tips
nodes.good_fits: Fitness, gym tips, discipline, nutrition, beginner transformation

nodes.label: user-aura-fit3-fitness--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "elite ball knowledge for becoming unrecognizable in 3 months"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Rapid physique promise followed by five numbered fundamentals: rep range, warmup/cardio timing, eating, lift tracking, consistency.
nodes.best_fit: Fitness beginners, muscle growth, gym discipline, workout tracking

nodes.label: user-aura-fit3-fitness--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "how to grow so much muscle in 3 months that it feels illegal..."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Hyperbolic curiosity hook makes the same five-tip muscle-growth template feel more viral and forbidden.
nodes.best_fit: Muscle growth, gym tips, discipline, beginner transformation

nodes.label: user-aura-fit3-fitness--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "5 life altering hacks to become unrecognizable in 3 months"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Life-hack phrasing packages supplements, pre-workout timing, paused reps, and accountability as quick transformation levers.
nodes.best_fit: Supplements, lifting technique, accountability, gym tips

nodes.label: journey-chronology
nodes.meaning: Uses a long before-to-after timeline as proof
nodes.good_fits: Personal brands, athlete accounts, founder stories

nodes.label: user-football-ashton-schumann--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "My football journey"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Long chronological proof arc from childhood through offers, commitment, redshirt, and transfer decision.
nodes.best_fit: Personal athlete accounts, recruiting stories, football progression

nodes.label: failure-confession
nodes.meaning: Starts from a mistake and turns it into advice
nodes.portable_formula: How I became [bad outcome], and how you avoid it

nodes.label: user-football-kingdubar--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "How I Became A D1 Bust. How To Make Sure You Don't Become One As Well."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Personal failure is turned into a mistake list and warning system.
nodes.best_fit: Football, athlete coaching, cautionary personal story
nodes.priority_reason: Strong failure-confession pattern for any personal or coaching account.

nodes.label: one-percent-contrast
nodes.meaning: Contrasts average behavior against elite behavior
nodes.portable_formula: Why most [group] never [goal]. The 1% do this differently

nodes.label: user-football-kingdubar--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "Why Most Athletes NEVER go D1. The 1% Do This Differently."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Average athlete habits are contrasted against top 1 percent behaviors.
nodes.best_fit: Football, recruiting advice, athlete standards

nodes.label: avoid-list
nodes.meaning: Frames actions as mistakes to avoid, even when the slides are positive prescriptions
nodes.portable_formula: [Number] things to avoid if you want [goal]

nodes.label: user-marcogetsfit-lifestyle--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "3 things you should AVOID if you wanna go pro."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Short avoid/do-this list with each slide showing a practical training behavior and payoff.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer, athlete training, go-pro advice

nodes.label: user-soccer-laviedem30--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "3 Things to avoid if you wanna go pro."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Avoid-list wrapper actually reveals positive pro-level physical habits.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer, training, strength, speed, recovery

nodes.label: user-soccer-laviedem30--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "3 Things you should AVOID if you wanna go pro."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Skill and mobility drills are framed as neglected requirements.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer, technical training, injury prevention

nodes.label: user-soccer-laviedem30--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "3 Things you should AVOID if you wanna go pro."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Strength, agility, and recovery behaviors are packaged as a pro-readiness checklist.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer, field training, athlete recovery

nodes.label: invisible-competition
nodes.meaning: Reminds viewers that someone else is working while they rest
nodes.portable_formula: While you [weak behavior], someone else [strong behavior]

nodes.label: user-soccer-tgaskins3--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "While you skipped your last rep, someone else did an extra one. Just so yk."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Every slide contrasts viewer laziness with another player's unseen discipline.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer mindset, athlete discipline, competitive pressure

nodes.label: tactical-how-to
nodes.meaning: Directly teaches a skill with sequential steps
nodes.good_fits: Sports training, study methods, business skills

nodes.label: user-soccer-tgaskins3--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "How to become an elite defender"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Tactical defender steps teach positioning, patience, pace, adaptation, and standards.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer defenders, skill tutorials, coaching content

nodes.label: user-soccer-tgaskins3--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "How to improve your game rapidly."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Simple improvement checklist with red-highlighted power words and pressure to double effort.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer players, skill improvement, youth athlete advice

nodes.label: identity-transformation
nodes.meaning: Sells a stronger identity, not just a behavior

nodes.label: user-soccer-tgaskins3--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "How to be a RUTHLESS KILLER on the pitch"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Charged identity hook converts tactical advice into an aggressive mindset code.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer mindset, confidence, competitive identity

nodes.label: user-soccer-tgaskins3--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "How to develop an EGO"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Reframes ego as belief, fearlessness, risk-taking, and chip-on-shoulder confidence.
nodes.best_fit: Soccer mindset, confidence, athlete identity

nodes.label: unusual-micro-habits
nodes.meaning: Uses small strange behaviors to make common advice feel fresh

nodes.label: user-marcogetsfit-lifestyle--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "Rewire your brain: 3 habits that kill laziness."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Small low-friction habits are framed as rewiring discipline.
nodes.best_fit: Lifestyle, discipline, productivity, anti-laziness

nodes.label: user-marcogetsfit-lifestyle--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "3 unusual evening habits that make you 1% stronger while everyone sleeps."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Strange nighttime actions create private accountability and identity advantage.
nodes.best_fit: Discipline, personal growth, athlete habits
nodes.priority_reason: Unusual micro-habit structure is portable and easy to adapt.

nodes.label: user-marcogetsfit-lifestyle--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "3 unusual morning habits that actually build discipline."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Morning friction tasks are sold as discipline training, not generic routine advice.
nodes.best_fit: Discipline, lifestyle, morning routines

nodes.label: authority-borrowed-proof
nodes.meaning: Borrows credibility from a professor, school, parent, grandparent, or institution

nodes.label: user-sophia-study-study--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "My dad is 2nd highest paid professor at Oxford and he taught me a brain hack that changed how I study forever."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Family/institution authority introduces one unconventional study hack.
nodes.best_fit: Study, student productivity, focus, authority-led advice

nodes.label: user-sophia-study-study--post-2
nodes.opening_hook: "My grandpa is the 2nd highest paid professor at Harvard, yet nobody believes him when he says these things."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Elder authority plus disbelief frame makes study advice feel hidden.
nodes.best_fit: Study, academic advice, elite school positioning

nodes.label: ranking-review
nodes.meaning: Reviews methods with scores after trying them

nodes.label: user-sophia-study-study--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "Ranking all the study methods I've tried as a neuroscience student at Harvard."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Methods are scored after claimed personal testing, creating comparison and debate.
nodes.best_fit: Study, productivity, method reviews
nodes.priority_reason: Ranking/review pattern that can be reused for methods, drills, tools, routines, or habits.

nodes.label: matcher-list
nodes.meaning: Matches situations to the right method

nodes.label: user-sophia-study-study--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "The most effective study method for each subject"
nodes.transferable_pattern: Each subject gets a matched method, reducing decision fatigue for the viewer.
nodes.best_fit: Study, exam prep, productivity systems

nodes.label: red-flag-fix
nodes.meaning: Names hidden signals and quick fixes

nodes.label: verified-byhumans--post-3
nodes.opening_hook: "Body language making men look weak, and the 2-second fixes."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Hidden social signals are named as weakness, then paired with fast fixes.
nodes.best_fit: Body language, confidence, dating, masculinity
nodes.priority_reason: Clean red-flag/fix structure usable across hygiene, confidence, and performance niches.

nodes.label: verified-byhumans--post-4
nodes.opening_hook: "Small details making men look unhygienic: the invisible red flags."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Tiny hygiene misses become invisible red flags with cheap practical fixes.
nodes.best_fit: Hygiene, self-presentation, men's lifestyle

nodes.label: biology-prevention
nodes.meaning: Warns that normal habits are damaging biological potential

nodes.label: verified-byhumans--post-1
nodes.opening_hook: "How to protect your genetics: habits destroying your body."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Everyday habits are framed as biological damage, then each slide gives what to avoid.
nodes.best_fit: Men's health, hygiene, focus, behavior change

nodes.label: verified-byhumans--post-5
nodes.opening_hook: "How to fix a slow brain and rebuild your focus."
nodes.transferable_pattern: Cognitive drains are explained as biological/attention problems with fix directions.
nodes.best_fit: Focus, brain health, productivity, men's self-improvement
